zUConvert an option to wire format.
        Returns a ``bytes`` or ``None``.

NotImplementedError
filer
to_wireE
Option.to_wirec
Build an EDNS option object from wire format.
        *otype*, an ``int``, is the option type.
        *parser*, a ``dns.wire.Parser``, the parser, which should be
        restructed to the option length.
        Returns a ``dns.edns.Option``.
        Nr(
parserr
from_wire_parserM
Option.from_wire_parserc
Compare an EDNS option with another option of the same type.
        Returns < 0 if < *other*, 0 if == *other*, and > 0 if > *other*.
        r

GenericOptionzwGeneric Option Class
    This class is used for EDNS option types for which we have no better
    implementation.
    c
superr'

GenericOption.to_wirec
Generic %d)

GenericOption.from_wire_parser)
__classcell__r
	ECSOptionz!EDNS Client Subnet (ECS, RFC7871)Nr
*address*, a ``str``, is the client address information.
        *srclen*, an ``int``, the source prefix length, which is the
        leftmost number of bits of the address to be used for the
        lookup.  The default is 24 for IPv4 and 56 for IPv6.
        *scopelen*, an ``int``, the scope prefix length.  This value
        must be 0 in queries, and should be set in responses.
        
Bad address family
inetZ
af_for_address
socketZ
AF_INET6
familyrI
_as_ipv6_addressZ
_as_intZ
AF_INETZ
_as_ipv4_address
ValueError
address
srclen
scopelenZ	inet_pton
math
ceil
addrdata
struct
pack
ord)	r&
afrc
nbytesZ
nbitsZ
lastrL
ECSOption.__init__c
ECS {}/{} scope/{})
formatr]
ECSOption.to_textc
Convert a string into a `dns.edns.ECSOption`
        *text*, a `str`, the text form of the option.
        Returns a `dns.edns.ECSOption`.
        Examples:
        >>> import dns.edns
        >>>
        >>> # basic example
        >>> dns.edns.ECSOption.from_text('1.2.3.4/24')
        >>>
        >>> # also understands scope
        >>> dns.edns.ECSOption.from_text('1.2.3.4/24/32')
        >>>
        >>> # IPv6
        >>> dns.edns.ECSOption.from_text('2001:4b98::1/64/64')
        >>>
        >>> # it understands results from `dns.edns.ECSOption.to_text()`
        >>> dns.edns.ECSOption.from_text('ECS 1.2.3.4/24/32')
        r
could not parse ECS from "{}"
invalid scope z
"{}": scope must be an integerz
invalid srclen z
"{}": srclen must be an integer)
split
lenr\
countr`
textZ
optional_prefix
tokensZ
ecs_textZ	n_slashesr]
scoper
	from_text

	EDEOptionz!Extended DNS Error (EDE, RFC8914)Nc
s4t	d
*code*, a ``dns.edns.EDECode`` or ``str``, the info code of the
        extended error.
        *text*, a ``str`` or ``None``, specifying additional information about
        the error.
        Nz
text must be string or None)
coder6
strr\

decode)
EDEOption.from_wire_parser)
Return the class for the specified option type.
    The GenericOption class is used if a more specific class is not
    known.
    N)
_type_to_class
getrF
get_option_class
Build an EDNS option object from wire format.
    *otype*, an ``int``, is the option type.
    *parser*, a ``dns.wire.Parser``, the parser, which should be
    restricted to the option length.
    Returns an instance of a subclass of ``dns.edns.Option``.
    )
option_from_wire_parser
Build an EDNS option object from wire format.
    *otype*, an ``int``, is the option type.
    *wire*, a ``bytes``, is the wire-format message.
    *current*, an ``int``, is the offset in *wire* of the beginning
    of the rdata.
    *olen*, an ``int``, is the length of the wire-format option data
    Returns an instance of a subclass of ``dns.edns.Option``.
    N)
ParserZ
restrict_tor
currentZ
olenr.
option_from_wire
Register the implementation of an option type.
    *implementation*, a ``class``, is a subclass of ``dns.edns.Option``.
    *otype*, an ``int``, is the option type.
    N)
implementationr%
register_type
) rE
dns.enumrH
